Title: Deciphering the role of monoacylglycerol lipases (MAGL) under abiotic stress and lipid metabolism in soybean

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: monoacylglycerol lipase family protein, α/β hydrolase domain
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: total seed oil; linolenic acid; oleic acid
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Significantly associated with total oil, linolenic acid, and oleic acid content
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene-specific association analysis using GLM/MLM; haplotyping/diversity analysis
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Positively correlated with Glyma.20G240600 and Glyma.06G087100 in co-expression analysis
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Predicted diverse MAGL localization; gene-specific site not directly validated
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing of 481 soybean accessions from SoyKB
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Glyma.10G291400 had the largest SNP number among soybean MAGLs. 2. It was significantly associated with total oil, linolenic acid, and oleic acid content. 3. It showed positive co-expression with Glyma.20G240600 and Glyma.06G087100. 4. The gene is a strong marker candidate for oil-quality traits.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Excellent candidate for marker-assisted selection and haplotype-based breeding for oil quantity and composition
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Strong multi-trait association but no direct functional assay in heterologous system reported

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: monoacylglycerol lipase family protein, α/β hydrolase domain
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strong association with oleic acid content by mixed linear model; also associated with linolenic acid in linear model; differentially expressed during seed developmental stages
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Differentially expressed across seed developmental stages
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing of 481 soybean accessions from SoyKB; soybean RNA-seq PRJNA388955, PRJNA288296, PRJNA399013, PRJNA197251, PRJNA384236
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Glyma.03G214800 was strongly associated with oleic acid content in mixed linear model analysis. 2. It also associated with linolenic acid in the linear model. 3. The gene showed differential expression across seed developmental stages, supporting a role in seed lipid metabolism.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising locus for improving soybean oil composition, especially oleic acid
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Cultivated-soybean Tajima's D minimum among listed MAGLs according to diversity table

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: monoacylglycerol lipase family protein, α/β hydrolase domain
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Significantly associated with total seed oil content by mixed linear model
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing of 481 soybean accessions from SoyKB
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Glyma.20G240600 was significantly associated with total seed oil content. 2. It showed positive co-expression with Glyma.10G291400. 3. The gene is a candidate locus for oil-content variation in soybean.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Located near previously identified oil QTLs on chromosome 20

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: monoacylglycerol lipase family protein, α/β hydrolase domain
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Seed-specific expression and hub-gene behavior; expression responsive to silicon/arsenic; overexpression in yeast reduced lipid-body fluorescence indicating altered lipid deposition
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq expression profiling; qRT-PCR under As/Si treatment; co-expression analysis; heterologous overexpression in yeast; confocal/FACS lipid assay
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Seed-specific expression; highest expression in leaves of Si-treated plants among qRT-PCR comparisons, downregulated under As and As+Si in leaf
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Altered lipid deposition in yeast overexpression assay
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Other MAGL genes in co-expression network
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Soybean RNA-seq PRJNA388955, PRJNA288296, PRJNA399013, PRJNA197251, PRJNA384236
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Glyma.05G248100 showed seed-specific expression. 2. It was selected as a hub gene for stress-responsive qRT-PCR analysis. 3. Its expression increased most in Si-treated leaves. 4. Overexpression in yeast altered lipid deposition, supporting a role in lipid metabolism.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for coupling oil metabolism with abiotic-stress response
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of four genes tested in yeast lipid-deposition assay

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: monoacylglycerol lipase family protein, α/β hydrolase domain
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Membrane lipid remodeling under arsenic/silicon stress
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Hub gene with strong differential expression under silicon and arsenic treatments, especially increased in roots with Si and stems with As
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq/microarray/co-expression analysis; qRT-PCR under As/Si treatment
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High in roots of Si-treated plants; highest in stem under As-treated plants; little expression in Si-treated leaf
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Soybean RNA-seq PRJNA388955, PRJNA288296, PRJNA399013, PRJNA197251, PRJNA384236
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Glyma.13G101400 was identified as a hub MAGL gene from RNA-seq, microarray, and co-expression analyses. 2. qRT-PCR showed strong treatment-responsive expression under silicon and arsenic. 3. The expression pattern supports a role in stress-associated lipid remodeling.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering abiotic-stress resilience through lipid remodeling
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as homolog of Arabidopsis stress-associated At1g52760

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Glycine max; Nicotiana tabacum; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: α/β hydrolase domain-containing MAGL family protein with GXSXG motif and catalytic triad S-D-H
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Triacylglycerol breakdown / lipid catabolism / fatty acid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Final step of TAG hydrolysis via MAG hydrolysis
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: downstream terminal step of TAG catabolism
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes monoacylglycerol to free fatty acid and glycerol in the last step of triacylglycerol breakdown
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: releases fatty acids from storage lipids and contributes to lipid remodeling
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: seed oil; oleic acid; linolenic acid; free fatty acids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Seed storage carbon/energy reserve; membrane lipid remodeling under abiotic stress
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression altered lipid deposition, producing smaller and more dispersed lipid droplets with reduced BODIPY fluorescence, supporting a role in lipid turnover/metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient GFP localization in tobacco; heterologous overexpression in yeast; confocal microscopy; FACS/BODIPY lipid staining; RNA-seq/co-expression/hub-gene analysis; qRT-PCR under As/Si treatment
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Hub gene; highly responsive in leaves under arsenic treatment; selected for qRT-PCR due to transcriptome and co-expression analyses
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Chloroplast-localized in tobacco leaves; altered lipid body pattern when overexpressed in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: MAGL co-expression network partners; DGAT/TAG-associated network inferred at family level
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Arsenic; silicon supplementation; abiotic stress including drought and heat
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S in tobacco; galactose-inducible promoter in pYES2.1 in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing of 481 soybean accessions from SoyKB; soybean RNA-seq PRJNA388955, PRJNA288296, PRJNA399013, PRJNA197251, PRJNA384236
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Glyma.13G183800 encodes a soybean MAGL-family lipase predicted to catalyze MAG hydrolysis. 2. The protein localized to chloroplasts in transient tobacco assays. 3. It was identified as a hub MAGL gene in co-expression analyses. 4. Its expression increased strongly in leaves under arsenic treatment. 5. Overexpression in yeast reduced lipid droplet size/fluorescence, indicating altered lipid deposition and turnover.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No clear growth effect in yeast stress survival assay under arsenic, mannitol, or NaCl; direct agronomic tradeoff in soybean not tested
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering seed oil composition/yield and stress-responsive lipid remodeling; useful for marker-assisted or haplotype-based breeding when combined with oil-content loci
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of the three MAGLs experimentally validated for chloroplast localization and one of four tested in yeast lipid-deposition assay

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Glycine max; Nicotiana tabacum; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: α/β hydrolase domain-containing MAGL family protein with GXSXG motif and catalytic triad S-D-H
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Triacylglycerol breakdown / lipid catabolism / fatty acid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Final step of TAG hydrolysis via MAG hydrolysis
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: downstream terminal step of TAG catabolism
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes monoacylglycerol to free fatty acid and glycerol in the last step of triacylglycerol breakdown
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: releases fatty acids from storage lipids and contributes to lipid remodeling
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: oleic acid; seed oil; free fatty acids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Seed storage reserve and membrane lipid remodeling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly associated with oleic acid content by mixed linear model; overexpression in yeast reduced lipid-body fluorescence and dispersion pattern, supporting a role in lipid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene-specific association analysis (MLM/GLM); transient GFP localization in tobacco; heterologous overexpression in yeast; confocal microscopy; FACS/BODIPY lipid staining; expression profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Exclusive expression in seedlings based on transcriptomic data
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Chloroplast-localized in tobacco leaves; altered lipid body pattern in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Abiotic stress transcriptome context; seedling stage
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S in tobacco; galactose-inducible promoter in pYES2.1 in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing of 481 soybean accessions from SoyKB; soybean RNA-seq PRJNA388955, PRJNA288296, PRJNA399013, PRJNA197251, PRJNA384236
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Glyma.09G184000 is a soybean MAGL-family lipase candidate. 2. It showed strong association with oleic acid content in the mixed linear model. 3. The protein localized to chloroplasts in tobacco leaves. 4. It displayed exclusive seedling expression in soybean transcriptome data. 5. Overexpression in yeast altered lipid droplet deposition, supporting a role in lipid metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No stress-growth phenotype detected in yeast survival assay; agronomic penalties not reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate gene for improving oleic acid-related traits and studying chloroplast lipid remodeling
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Among MAGLs with experimentally validated subcellular localization and yeast lipid assay support

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: α/β hydrolase domain-containing MAGL family protein with GXSXG motif and catalytic triad S-D-H
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Triacylglycerol breakdown / lipid catabolism / fatty acid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Final step of TAG hydrolysis via MAG hydrolysis
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: downstream terminal step of TAG catabolism
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes monoacylglycerol to free fatty acid and glycerol in the last step of triacylglycerol breakdown
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: releases fatty acids and may contribute to membrane lipid remodeling
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Storage oil mobilization and stress-related lipid remodeling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Seed-specific and drought-responsive expression plus chloroplast localization support a role in seed lipid metabolism and stress-associated lipid remodeling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient GFP localization in tobacco; RNA-seq expression profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Seed-specific expression; differential expression under drought; identified among hub genes in co-expression discussion
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Soybean RNA-seq PRJNA388955, PRJNA288296, PRJNA399013, PRJNA197251, PRJNA384236
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Glyma.08G056600 is a MAGL-family lipase candidate expressed specifically in seed. 2. It showed differential expression under drought. 3. The protein localized to chloroplasts in tobacco leaves. 4. The results support a role in seed lipid metabolism and stress-related membrane remodeling.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for improving seed oil metabolism together with abiotic stress responsiveness
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Hydrophobic by GRAVY compared with most MAGLs according to supplementary description

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Williams 82 haplotype present in major haplotype group
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: α/β hydrolase MAGL family protein with catalytic triad S-D-H
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Triacylglycerol breakdown / lipid catabolism / fatty acid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Final step of TAG hydrolysis via MAG hydrolysis
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: downstream terminal step of TAG catabolism
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes monoacylglycerol to free fatty acid and glycerol
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: releases fatty acids from storage oil and influences oil content
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: total seed oil; free fatty acids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Seed storage reserve and lipid turnover
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Significantly associated with total seed oil content by mixed linear model; mutant haplotypes include stop-gain and deleterious variants affecting catalytic motifs; overexpression in yeast altered lipid deposition
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene-specific association analysis; haplotyping; SNP functional effect prediction; protein alignment; heterologous overexpression in yeast; confocal/FACS lipid assay
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Altered lipid body deposition in yeast overexpression assay
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Predicted chloroplast-related homologous localization discussed; direct transient validation not shown for this gene in Results
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: galactose-inducible promoter in pYES2.1 in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing of 481 soybean accessions from SoyKB
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Glyma.10G273600 was significantly associated with total seed oil content. 2. It showed extensive haplotypic variation including a stop-gain mutation causing truncation and loss of D/H catalytic motifs. 3. Several deleterious missense variants were predicted. 4. The gene co-expressed with DGAT2D. 5. Overexpression in yeast altered lipid deposition, supporting a role in lipid metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss-of-function alleles may impair catalytic activity; direct yield or stress penalty in soybean not tested
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for haplotype-based breeding and functional marker development for seed oil traits
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Major haplotype group included Williams 82; one of four genes tested in yeast lipid assay

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: α/β hydrolase MAGL family protein with catalytic triad S-D-H
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Triacylglycerol breakdown / lipid catabolism / fatty acid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Final step of TAG hydrolysis via MAG hydrolysis
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: downstream terminal step of TAG catabolism
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes monoacylglycerol to free fatty acid and glycerol
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Significantly associated with total seed oil content using mixed linear model; identified as hub gene in co-expression analysis discussion
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Hub-gene tendency based on co-expression/RNA-seq discussion
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing of 481 soybean accessions from SoyKB; soybean RNA-seq PRJNA388955, PRJNA288296, PRJNA399013, PRJNA197251, PRJNA384236
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Glyma.20G116400 was significantly associated with total seed oil content in mixed linear model analysis. 2. It was highlighted among hub MAGL genes in co-expression analyses. 3. The gene is a strong candidate contributing to soybean oil metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful marker candidate for oil-content breeding
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as homolog of Arabidopsis At1g77420 together with Glyma.10G273600
